cards.back: A number that represents part of a whole: numerator / denominator.

cards.front: What does the denominator tell you?
cards.back: How many equal parts the whole is divided into.

cards.front: Give an example of comparing 1/2 and 1/3 using real-world slices.
cards.back: A pizza cut into 2 pieces gives larger slices than the same pizza cut into 3 pieces — each half is bigger than each third.
Same numerator; the smaller denominator means fewer, therefore larger, parts — so 1/2 > 1/3.
Halves split the whole into fewer parts than thirds, so each half occupies more of the whole than each third.

cards.front: Common mistake: treating bigger denominators as bigger fractions. Why is that wrong?
cards.back: A larger denominator means more slices of the same whole, so each slice is smaller — the fraction's value goes down when the denominator grows (with the same numerator).
